prose:
Calculation summary (method-provenance block)
Property: HOMO–LUMO (Koopmans) gap, in electron-volts (eV)
Level of theory: DFT, B3LYP/def2-TZVP (no density fitting; exact four-center integrals)
Geometry provenance: user-supplied file ‘water.xyz’ (3 atoms); single-point calculation — coordinates not re-optimized at DFT (notation: DFT//input geometry)
Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvation)
Total charge = 0 Multiplicity = 1 (closed-shell singlet); treated with restricted Kohn–Sham (RKS)
Software/backend: PySCF (invoked via chemkit frontier-orbitals skill)
SCF convergence: achieved in 7 cycles; SCF converged = true
Engine warnings: no warnings reported
Live log: the calculation was streamed to a `.out` file in the working directory during execution (path shown at launch in the session transcript).

Key results
HOMO energy = −8.6037183 eV
LUMO energy = +0.33695037 eV
HOMO–LUMO gap = 8.9406687 eV

The headline HOMO–LUMO gap (8.9406687 eV) is the value returned by chemkit (frontier-orbitals, B3LYP/def2-TZVP) on the provided water geometry. No warnings were issued; integrity check reports trustworthy = true.
